query: is obesity related to cognitive decline

retrieved_docs.title: History Taking in Obesity Medicine: A Comprehensive Guide -- Issues of Concern -- Cognitive assessment considerations
retrieved_docs.content: Cognitive function assessment is essential in obesity medicine due to its potential impact on treatment adherence, decision-making, and the success of lifestyle modifications (see Table 19 ). [30]

retrieved_docs.title: Cognitive Deficits -- Deterrence and Patient Education
retrieved_docs.content: Apart from increasing age, hypertension, diabetes, hypercholesterolemia, smoking, and obesity are thought to be responsible for the clearance of amyloid (protein) from the brain, which in turn increases the risk of developing Alzheimer disease. A higher risk of Alzheimer disease, in particular, is associated with the presence of a number of these risk factors at the same time and while the person is in his or her 50s. So it is essential to maintain healthy blood pressure, blood sugar, and blood cholesterol.

retrieved_docs.title: Geriatric Cognitive Decline and Polypharmacy -- Etiology
retrieved_docs.content: The risk factors for polypharmacy-related mild cognitive impairment are age over 65, multiple comorbidities including hypertension, diabetes, obesity, Apo ε4 carriers, cerebrovascular accidents, low socio-economic status, and lower education. Any increase in the number of medications prescribed to individuals raises the risk of unexpected drug interactions and their side effects, leading to impaired cognitive or physical capability. Over time, the cumulative exposure of multiple drugs with changes in body homeostasis with age makes the elderly vulnerable to pronounced mental and physical decline. [4]

retrieved_docs.title: Obesity Medications: Evidence-Based Management -- Clinical Significance -- Neuropsychiatric adverse effects
retrieved_docs.content: Cognitive function assessment: Conduct systematic evaluation of cognitive function, including attention, memory, word-finding ability, and processing speed, at baseline and every 3 months. When clinically indicated, use standardized tools, eg, the Montreal Cognitive Assessment or Mini-Mental State Examination. Monitor for reports of difficulty concentrating, confusion, or "brain fog."

retrieved_docs.title: Psychological Issues Associated With Obesity -- Differential Diagnosis
retrieved_docs.content: Cognitive functioning and executive dysfunction differential diagnoses : Attention-deficit/hyperactivity disorder (ADHD), mild cognitive impairment, neurocognitive disorders (eg, due to medical conditions such as hypothyroidism or neurodegenerative diseases).

retrieved_docs.title: Obesity and Comorbid Conditions -- Issues of Concern -- Psychiatric Disorders
retrieved_docs.content: The incidence of depression is higher in patients with obesity, especially among young women. Additionally, an association between obesity and dementia has been observed; however, the mechanism remains poorly understood, and whether obesity constitutes an independent risk factor for dementia is uncertain. [57] [58] [59] [60]

retrieved_docs.title: Alzheimer Disease -- Etiology
retrieved_docs.content: Obesity and diabetes are also important modifiable risk factors for AD. Obesity can impair glucose tolerance and increase the risk of developing type II diabetes. Chronic hyperglycemia can lead to cognitive impairment by promoting the accumulation of beta-amyloid (A-beta) and neuroinflammation. Obesity further amplifies the risk by triggering the release of pro-inflammatory cytokines and promoting insulin resistance. [10]

retrieved_docs.title: Mild Cognitive Impairment -- Deterrence and Patient Education
retrieved_docs.content: Currently, there are no treatments that can reduce the risk of MCI. However, control of vascular risk factors may be important. The treatment of hypertension might reduce the risk of developing MCI and the composite result of MCI and probable dementia. [42] Treating other vascular risk factors, such as diabetes mellitus, hyperlipidemia, and obesity, should follow the cardiovascular and cerebrovascular prevention guidelines. [43] Concurrent treatment is required for conditions such as OSA, depression, hypothyroidism, polypharmacy, and hearing impairment. [43]

retrieved_docs.title: Behavioral Approaches to Obesity Treatment -- Other Issues
retrieved_docs.content: Cognitive therapy: Some patients with obesity may verbalize thoughts lacking in validity, known as cognitive distortions. Cognitive therapy targets these cognitive distortions and guides them towards less maladaptive thinking, behavior, and emotional reactions. Compared with MI, the cognitive shift is the key mechanism of change.

retrieved_docs.title: Major Neurocognitive Disorder (Dementia) (Nursing) -- Risk Factors
retrieved_docs.content: Although there are many causes of dementia, certain risk factors have been associated with dementia and cognitive decline. Evidence shows that the greatest risk factor for late-onset dementia is age, family history, and genetic susceptibility. However, some modifiable risk factors such as uncontrolled diabetes, mid-life obesity, hypertension, hyperlipidemia, smoking, and history of traumatic brain injury have been known to also be associated with the development of dementia. [7]

retrieved_docs.title: Special Populations and Long-Term Management in Obesity Medicine -- Function -- Complex medical populations
retrieved_docs.content: Severe mental illness : Patients with conditions such as schizophrenia or bipolar disorder face unique challenges, including medication-induced weight gain, cognitive barriers to behavior change, and social determinants affecting food access. Collaborative care with psychiatry and social services is essential.

retrieved_docs.title: Mild Cognitive Impairment -- Etiology
retrieved_docs.content: There are various risk factors for MCI.

retrieved_docs.title: Mild Cognitive Impairment -- Etiology -- Metabolic or Infectious Causes
retrieved_docs.content: Hypothyroidism, vitamin B12 deficiency, dehydration, hypo or hyperglycemia, and infections such as urinary tract infections can all cause cognitive impairment and are easily treated. [6]

retrieved_docs.title: Obesity Medications: Evidence-Based Management -- Clinical Significance -- Neuropsychiatric adverse effects
retrieved_docs.content: Dose optimization strategies: If cognitive impairment or mood changes develop, consider reducing the dose to the lowest effective level. Some patients may benefit from alternate-day dosing or extended-release formulations to minimize peak concentration effects. Discontinuation may be necessary if symptoms significantly impact daily functioning or quality of life.

retrieved_docs.title: Mild Cognitive Impairment -- Treatment / Management
retrieved_docs.content: Treatment of reversible causes should be prioritized; conditions such as OSA, depression, hypothyroidism, vitamin deficiency, and polypharmacy are easily treated and may lead to the reversal of cognitive impairment. Vascular risk factors such as hyperlipidemia, hypertension, diabetes mellitus, atrial fibrillation, and tobacco and alcohol use should be addressed to slow down ischemic damage.

retrieved_docs.title: Mild Cognitive Impairment -- Etiology -- Chronic Medical Conditions and Multimorbidity
retrieved_docs.content: Vascular risk factors such as hypertension, hyperlipidemia, coronary artery disease, and stroke are associated with MCI. A study focused on multimorbidity and MCI showed that patients with 4 or more comorbid conditions, especially 2 of the following: hypertension, coronary artery disease, hyperlipidemia, and osteoarthritis, have an increased risk of MCI. [6] [8] Other chronic diseases, such as chronic obstructive pulmonary disease and diabetes mellitus, are also risk factors for MCI. [6]

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 14 -- Level 2 -- Community 1088
retrieved_docs.content: Chronic inflammatory small bowel disease is described as having a chronic clinical course. This chronic course can lead to secondary diabetes mellitus accompanied by diabetic chronic kidney disease, and it is also associated with chronic kidney disease (قصور كلوي مزمن). Feigenbaum Bergeron Richardson syndrome is linked to impaired interpretation, indicating that patients with this syndrome may experience deficits in interpreting information. The state of impairment is further associated with dementia, senile cognitive decline, and acute confusional states, as well as with a social communication disorder (اضطراب التواصل الاجتماعي (العملي)). Impaired interpretation is also connected to chronic kidney disease (قصور كلوي مزمن), suggesting that renal dysfunction may contribute to or be affected by cognitive and interpretive deficits. This network of relationships highlights the interplay between chronic gastrointestinal inflammation, metabolic and renal complications, and neurocognitive impairments in certain syndromes.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 21 -- Level 3 -- Community 2348
retrieved_docs.content: استسقاء الدماغ (hydrocephalus) is classified as a subcategory of both “Brain and Nerves” and the parent term hlava, indicating its placement within central nervous system disorders. Clinically, استسقاء الدماغ is associated with خرف (dementia) and خرف شيخوخي (senile or age‑related dementia), highlighting the importance of monitoring cognitive function in patients with hydrocephalus and addressing potential neurodegenerative complications as part of comprehensive care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 19 -- Level 3 -- Community 1519
retrieved_docs.content: Chronic inflammatory small bowel disease is characterized by a chronic clinical course, which can progress to secondary diabetes mellitus accompanied by diabetic chronic kidney disease and may also culminate in chronic kidney failure (قصور كلوي مزمن). Feigenbaum Bergeron Richardson syndrome impairs the ability to interpret information; this impairment is associated with a practical social communication disorder (اضطراب التواصل الاجتماعي (العملي)) and extends to a distorted interpretation of chronic kidney failure. Together, these relationships highlight how a persistent inflammatory bowel condition can predispose patients to metabolic and renal complications, while Feigenbaum Bergeron Richardson syndrome contributes to cognitive‑communication deficits that may further affect the perception and management of renal disease.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 34 -- Level 5 -- Community 2941
retrieved_docs.content: Diet therapy is recognized as an appropriate and permissible intervention for several distinct medical conditions. It can be employed as part of the management strategy for الخرف من نوع الزهايمر (Alzheimer‑type dementia), where tailored nutritional plans may support cognitive function and overall health. In thyroid disorders, dietary modifications are allowed to help regulate metabolism and complement pharmacologic treatment. For تضيُّق الصمام المترالي (mitral valve stenosis), diet therapy is permitted as a supportive measure to optimize cardiovascular health and reduce symptom burden. Additionally, diet therapy is considered acceptable for فالج (paralysis), providing nutritional support that can aid in maintaining muscle mass, preventing complications, and enhancing overall patient well‑being. Integrating diet therapy into the care of these conditions underscores the role of nutrition in comprehensive medical management.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2312
retrieved_docs.content: استسقاء الدماغ (hydrocephalus) is situated within the broader category of Brain and Nerves and is identified as a member of the Neurological Disorders (PsycINFO Subcluster Term). Clinically, استسقاء الدماغ is associated with خرف شيخوخي (senile dementia), indicating a link between ventricular enlargement and age‑related cognitive decline. Recognizing استسقاء الدماغ as part of the Brain and Nerves hierarchy and its connection to خرف شيخوخي assists clinicians in addressing both structural and functional neurological aspects when evaluating patients.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 25 -- Level 3 -- Community 1873
retrieved_docs.content: treatment of any infectious component. Injuries and infections are evaluated by physical examination and microbiological testing; etiology spans mechanical trauma and bacterial, viral or parasitic pathogens; complications may progress to sepsis, chronic wounds, organ dysfunction or disability; pathology demonstrates tissue damage, necrosis and inflammatory infiltrates; management is tailored to the specific injury or pathogen with wound care, antibiotics, antiparasitics, antivirals, supportive measures and rehabilitation. Alzheimer‑type dementia is diagnosed through cognitive testing, neuroimaging and biomarkers; etiology involves amyloid‑beta accumulation, tau pathology, genetic risk factors and vascular contributions; complications include loss of independence, behavioral disturbances, caregiver burden and increased mortality; parasitology is not implicated; pathology is characterized by neurofibrillary tangles, amyloid plaques and neuronal loss; therapy focuses on symptomatic

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 9 -- Level 3 -- Community 1702
retrieved_docs.content: Computerized tomography of brain and chest and Computerized tomography of brain and chest with intravenous contrast medium are both primary mappings of the computerized axial tomography (CT) scan head. The CT head examination is clinically associated with خرف (dementia) and commonly co‑occurs with خرف شيخوخي (senile dementia), indicating its relevance in the evaluation and management of cognitive decline, particularly age‑related dementia.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 23 -- Level 3 -- Community 2399
retrieved_docs.content: Physical & Psychosomatic Disorders (PsycINFO Subcluster Term) includes استسقاء الدماغ as one of its constituent conditions. استسقاء الدماغ is clinically associated with خرف and also with خرف شيخوخي, indicating that hydrocephalus can be linked to the development of dementia, including senile dementia. This relationship underscores the importance of monitoring cognitive function in patients with استسقاء الدماغ and integrating neurological and psychiatric assessment into their care plan.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 11 -- Level 2 -- Community 828
retrieved_docs.content: Computerized axial tomography (CT) scan head serves as the primary reference for more extensive contrast‑enhanced examinations, including computerized tomography of the brain and chest with intravenous contrast medium and computerized tomography of the brain, chest and abdomen with intravenous contrast medium. These head CT studies are clinically associated with خرف (dementia) and frequently co‑occur with خرف شيخوخي (senile or age‑related dementia). Consequently, contrast‑enhanced CT imaging of the brain—often performed together with chest and abdominal evaluation—plays a key role in the diagnostic assessment and monitoring of patients with dementia, particularly the senile form, highlighting the importance of head CT in the work‑up of cognitive decline.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 13 -- Level 2 -- Community 1250
retrieved_docs.content: Neurological Disorders (PsycINFO Subcluster Term) and Physical & Psychosomatic Disorders (PsycINFO Subcluster Term) both include استسقاء الدماغ as a member condition. استسقاء الدماغ, commonly known as hydrocephalus, is clinically associated with خرف شيخوخي, or senile dementia. This relationship highlights that hydrocephalus, while classified under neurological and physical/psychosomatic disorder categories, may contribute to or coexist with age‑related cognitive decline, underscoring the importance of recognizing its potential impact on dementia in patient assessment and management.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 15 -- Level 3 -- Community 1751
retrieved_docs.content: Feigenbaum Bergeron Richardson syndrome is linked to an impaired ability to interpret information, as reflected by the concept of “interpretation_of.” This impairment is manifested in several clinical contexts: it is associated with dementia, senile type with an acute confused state, it is interpreted as اضطراب التواصل الاجتماعي (العملي) (social communication disorder, practical), and it also relates to قصور كلوي مزمن (chronic kidney failure). The syndrome’s effect on interpretation therefore connects neurocognitive decline, acute confusion, functional communication deficits, and renal insufficiency, underscoring the importance of integrated evaluation and management of these interrelated conditions.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 2 -- Level 3 -- Community 2498
retrieved_docs.content: استسقاء الدماغ (hydrocephalus) is classified within the broader domain of Brain and Nerves and is also listed as a member of the Neurological Disorders (PsycINFO Subcluster Term). Clinically, استسقاء الدماغ is associated with خرف (dementia) and خرف شيخوخي (senile dementia), indicating that patients with hydrocephalus may be at risk for developing these forms of cognitive impairment.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 1 -- Level 3 -- Community 2515
retrieved_docs.content: Hydrocephalus (استسقاء الدماغ) is a neurological disorder that pertains to the head (hlava) and is clinically associated with dementia (خرف), including senile dementia (خرف شيخوخي). This connection indicates that individuals with hydrocephalus may experience cognitive decline and develop various forms of dementia, underscoring the importance of monitoring neurocognitive function in patients with this condition.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 21 -- Level 2 -- Community 1234
retrieved_docs.content: Senility and organic mental disorders is a broad diagnostic category that encompasses several related conditions. It includes a child entity described as “dementie; seniel, met acute verwardheidstoestand,” indicating a form of dementia in the elderly that may present with an acute confused or delirious state. Within this overarching category, Senility and organic mental disorders classifies schizophrenia and other psychotic disorders, linking late‑life psychosis to the broader spectrum of organic mental illness. It is also classified as “خرف وعائي مصحوب حالة مزاجية اكتئابية,” which refers to vascular dementia accompanied by a depressive mood, highlighting the frequent coexistence of cerebrovascular pathology, cognitive decline, and mood disturbance in older adults. Another classification, “ذُهان شيخوخي,” denotes senile psychosis, emphasizing that psychotic features can arise as a direct manifestation of age‑related brain changes. Additionally, Senility and organic mental disorders is classified as “ضمور المخ المحدد,” a term for specific brain atrophy, underscoring the role of structural neurodegeneration in the development of these conditions. The emotional blunting (“تبلد المشاعر”) observed in affected patients is identified as a manifestation of this specific brain atrophy, illustrating how neuroanatomical deterioration can lead to reduced affective responsiveness. Together, these interrelated concepts portray senility‑related organic mental disorders as a spectrum that includes dementia with acute confusion, vascular dementia with depression, late‑life psychosis, and distinct patterns of brain atrophy, all of which contribute to the clinical presentation and guide comprehensive patient care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 33 -- Level 3 -- Community 2511
retrieved_docs.content: Hydrocephalus (استسقاء الدماغ) is recognized as a neurological disorder and is listed as a member of the “Neurological Disorders (PsycINFO Subcluster Term)” category. It is a condition that pertains to the domain of “Brain and Nerves,” indicating that it affects the central nervous system, and it is also anatomically related to the head (hlava). Clinically, hydrocephalus is associated with senile dementia (خرف شيخوخي), underscoring its relevance in the evaluation of cognitive decline and the importance of integrating its assessment within broader neurological and neuro‑cognitive care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 9 -- Level 2 -- Community 682
retrieved_docs.content: Late effect of nervous system injury is identified as متلازمة ما بعد الارتجاج المخي (post‑concussion syndrome). The diagnostic grouping Senility and organic mental disorders is also classified as متلازمة ما بعد الارتجاج المخي, indicating that post‑concussion syndrome can be viewed within the broader context of age‑related and organic mental conditions. In addition, Senility and organic mental disorders is classified as خرف وعائي مصحوب حالة مزاجية اكتئابية (vascular dementia accompanied by depressive mood) and as ذُهان شيخوخي (senile psychosis), reflecting its inclusion of both cognitive decline with mood disturbance and psychotic features of old age. Moreover, this category classifies schizophrenia and other psychotic disorders, linking severe adult‑onset psychoses to the spectrum of senile and organic mental disorders. Together, these relationships illustrate that late neurological injury, vascular dementia with depression, senile psychosis, and schizophrenia are interconnected under the umbrella of senility‑related organic mental disorders, emphasizing the importance of recognizing overlapping symptomatology and shared pathophysiology in patient assessment and management.
